reasoning: NDAQ will undergo an ex-dividend date on March 16, 2026, for a $0.27 quarterly dividend. Under T+1 settlement rules, the ex-dividend date effectively aligns with the March 16 record date, meaning the stock will face an automatic $0.27 price drag (about 0.32%) between the March 13 closing price (used for the March 15 benchmark) and the March 20 closing price (used for March 22). Given weekly market drift is usually +0.15% to +0.20%, this dividend push flips the expected return over this week to slightly negative. Combined with recent short-term negative momentum (dropping from ~87.8 to 85.26 over the last week), the probability of the March 22 close price exceeding the March 15 close is slightly below a coin flip, at approximately 45%.
